# Scene

A six-panel comic strip featuring three plain white birds and one colorful bird with bright red plumage and green head markings, drawn in simple black and white line art (with color for the distinctive red bird). The birds are perched on a horizontal line representing a branch or wire. In the fifth panel, a black bird of prey swoops down.

# Text

**Panel 1:**
- Plain bird 1: "SO THEN I STARTED SINGING MY BEST SONG AT THE TOP OF MY LUNGS AND..."
- Plain bird 2: "WHA'SUP, VIRGINS?"

**Panel 2:**
- Red bird: "JESUS, CARL! WHAT'S WITH ALL THE FANCY PLUMAGE?"
- Plain bird 1: "IT'S CALLED EVOLU-TION. WELCOME TO BIRD-OF-PARADISE 2.0."

**Panel 3:**
- Red bird: "IF YOU WANNA GET LAID, YOU GOTTA BE SEEN. KNOW WHAT I'M SAYIN'?"

**Panel 4:**
- Red bird: "AND WHILE YOU LOSERS ARE SITTING HERE WITH YOUR DRAB COLORS, I'M OUT THERE GETTIN'..."

**Panel 5:**
(No dialogue)

**Panel 6:**
(No dialogue)

# Visual note

The red bird's bright, highly visible plumage—which it claims attracts mates—makes it an easy target for predators. It is captured immediately after boasting about its success, leaving only the camouflaged plain birds behind.

comics.transcript:
# Scene

A black and white courtroom depicted in a grid of panels with minimalist stick-figure art. Each panel shows a judge's bench with nine figures seated behind it and counsel tables below with three figures on each side. The comic escalates from logical mathematical argument to emotional rhetoric, with the final verdict replacing reason with outrage.

# Text

**Panel 1:**
[Mathematical equations on chalkboard behind judge]
P(Ω)={1}}135.2
k₂(2g,s)·s=s·s, β=σp_g·3>a⊗β·2...=σnβ=Λ
k₂(3),s|1,34 +3D1.3†, Fᵥₚₚ

**Panel 2:**
FROM THIS PROPOSITION IT WILL FOLLOW, WHEN ARITHMETICAL ADDITION HAS BEEN DEFINED, THAT 1+1=2.

**Panel 3:**
THANK YOU, COUNSELOR. LET'S HEAR THE OPPOSING ARGUMENT.

**Panel 4:**
I AM OUTRAGED AND APPALLED AT THE OPPOSING SIDE'S PERVERSE USE OF LOGIC AND MATH!

**Panel 5:**
THIS IS AN INSULT TO OUR GREAT COUNTRY, OUR BRAVE TROOPS, AND OUR FAMILY VALUES!!!

**Panel 6:**
WE SAY THAT 1+1=3!!!

**Panel 7:**
AND IF YOU DON'T LIKE IT, YOU SUNBITCHES CAN GET THE HELL OUTTA THE COUNTRY!!!

**Panel 8:**
THANK YOU, COUNSELORS. THE COURT WILL NOW DELIBERATE ON THE MERITS OF THE CASE.

**Panel 9:**
IT IS THE OPINION OF THIS COURT THAT EXTREME MORAL OUTRAGE IS MORE EFFECTIVE THAN DEDUCTIVE REASONING.

**Panel 10:**
THE COURT HAS CONCLUDED, 5 TO 4, THAT 1 PLUS 1 CAN INDEED EQUAL 3 IF YOU'RE ANGRY ENOUGH.

**Panel 11:**
AND, BY EXTENSION, THE USUAL ORDER RELATION ON THE INTEGERS IS EFFECTIVELY RENDERED MEANINGLESS. IT IS SO ORDERED.

**Panel 12:**
WHOO HOO! WHOO HOO!
THIS IS AN OUTRAGE.

# Visual note

The verdict replaces mathematical proof with emotional intensity as the basis for legal judgment, culminating in a ruling that emotion can override mathematical truth.

# Scene
A six-panel stick-figure webcomic in minimalist black line drawing. A young figure makes increasingly ambitious proclamations about future conquest, only to be interrupted by their mother with practical life advice. The final panel reveals the adult figure in a spacecraft labeled as Genghis Khan, Attorney-at-Law.

# Text

**Panel 1:**
"SOME DAY, I WILL BE A GREAT LEADER."

**Panel 2:**
"SOME DAY, I WILL COMMAND THE GREATEST ARMY THE WORLD HAS EVER SEEN."

**Panel 3:**
"AND I WILL CREATE THE LARGEST CONTIGUOUS EMPIRE IN HISTORY!"

**Panel 4:**
"AND THE EARTH WILL TREMBLE IN FEAR AT THE VERY SOUND OF MY NAME!!"

**Panel 5:**
"IT IS MY DESTINY!!!"

**Panel 5 (interruption):**
"DON'T BE RIDICULOUS. YOU'LL BE A LAWYER LIKE A GOOD MONGOL BOY. AND THAT'S THAT!"

**Panel 6:**
GENGHIS KHAN —ATTORNEY-AT-LAW

TIGER MOM
destroyer of empires

# Visual Note
The spacecraft resembles a UFO, contrasting the historical figure's legendary military conquests with an absurd bureaucratic profession, emphasizing the mother's power to redirect ambitious dreams toward practical outcomes.

# Webcomic Transcription

**Scene:** A four-panel comic in simple black-and-white stick figure style, followed by a large silhouette illustration. The first three panels depict a figure in a UFO experiencing escalating chaos, while the final image shows a silhouetted horseman on a hillside.

**Text:** No dialogue or captions.

**Visual note:** The comic presents an escalating sequence of dangers: a peaceful alien abduction, ejection from the saucer, being dropped by hands, attack by a mounted warrior, and finally a horseman being struck by lightning. The progression subverts expectations by suggesting that ordinary earthly hazards pose greater danger than extraterrestrial encounter.

comics.transcript:
# Webcomic Transcription

## Scene
A multi-panel webcomic in simple stick-figure style. The left side shows escalating scenes of an angel and smaller character, culminating in fire. The right side depicts two characters in dialogue against a pastoral farm setting, with black, white, green, and orange flame effects throughout.

## Text

**Left Column:**
- "BEST BY 13,814,866,948"
- "Lord, this universe is about to go bad."
- "hmmm. So it is."
- "Throw it in the incinerator."
- "Yes, Lord."

**Right Column:**
- "I LOVE WAITING FOR THE SUN TO RISE EVERY MORNING."
- "DID YOU EVER HEAR THE STORY OF THE INDUCTIVIST TURKEY?"
- "WHAT?"
- "IT'S A STORY THAT IS OFTEN CITED TO ILLUSTRATE THE PROBLEM OF INDUCTION."
- "Y'SEE, ON ITS FIRST DAY ON THE FARM, THE TURKEY GOT FED AT EXACTLY 1 PM."
- "SO NATURALLY THE TURKEY CONCLUDED THAT IT WOULD GET FED AT 1 AM EVERY DAY."
- "AND EVERY DAY FOR THE NEXT SEVERAL MONTHS THE TURKEY GOT FED AT 1 AM."
- "BUT THANKSGIVING ARRIVED AND IT GOT ITS THROAT CUT."
- "A REASONABLE TURKEY."
- "WHAT A HORRIBLE STORY."

## Visual Note
The "best by" date is an absurdly large number, suggesting the universe itself has an expiration date. The turkey's logical induction—that daily feeding means permanent safety—is undercut by its fate on Thanksgiving.

# Scene

A minimalist stick-figure comic with black backgrounds. Two characters sit across from each other at a table: one with black hair on the left, one depicted as a simple circle on the right. The art style uses simple line drawings with white text on black panels.

# Text

Panel 1:
"HMMM, I SENSE THAT ELVIS IS TRYING TO CONTACT YOU FROM THE BEYOND."

Panel 2:
"WOW, REALLY? FROM BEYOND WHERE?"
"FROM BEYOND WHERE? THIS DIMENSION. HE NOW EXISTS IN HIGHER DIMENSIONS."

Panel 3:
"HIGHER DIMENSIONS?"
"YES."

Panel 4:
"HIGHER DIMENSIONS OF VIBRATIONS. I CAN COMMUNICATE WITH HIM BY ATTUNING MY VIBRATIONAL FREQUENCY WITH HIS."

Panel 5:
"VIBRATIONAL FREQUENCY?"
"YES. MY LIFE-FORCE ENERGY IS VIBRATING."

Panel 6:
"LIFE. FORCE. ENERGY."
"WHAT?"

Panel 7:
(Silent)

Panel 8:
"YOU KEEP BORROWING A LOT OF WORDS FROM SCIENCE."

Panel 9:
"I DO NOT THINK THEY MEAN TO ME WHAT THEY MEAN TO YOU. ELVIS IS EXPERIENCING A GAUGE ANOMALY. YOU GO NOW."

# Visual Note

The humor relies on the character confidently using scientific terminology in ways that have no basis in actual science, then admitting the words mean something entirely different to them, followed by an abrupt dismissal.

# Scene
A four-panel black and white webcomic in simple stick-figure style. The first three panels depict a business meeting with four figures seated at a table. The final panel shows a large wall-mounted device covered in camera lenses and USB ports, with a crowd of stick figures below staring at it in shock.

# Text

**Panel 1:**
"SIR, WE HEAR RUMORS THAT THE GALAXY S10+ WILL FEATURE A TOTAL OF FIVE CAMERAS."
"FIVE?!!"

**Panel 2:**
"THOSE BASTARDS ARE OVERCOMPENSATING FOR SOMETHING. BUT I'M NOT GONNA LET THEM KICK OUR ASS."

**Panel 3:**
"LET'S SEE HOW MANY CAMERAS WE CAN PACK INTO OUR NEXT PHONE, AND WHILE YOU'RE AT IT, SEE HOW MANY USB PORTS YOU CAN FIT ON IT."
"RIGHT AWAY, SIR."

**Panel 4:**
(No text)

# Visual note
The executives' instructions result in a wall-mounted device absurdly overloaded with cameras and USB ports. The horrified crowd below emphasizes the satire of manufacturers escalating their feature count without practical purpose.

**Scene:**
A six-panel stick figure comic in black and white showing a person reacting to video game platform exclusives. Each panel depicts the stick figure's varying reactions, with product boxes appearing in later panels.

**Text:**

Panel 1:
THE LEGEND OF ZELDA BREATH OF THE WILD

Panel 2:
MAN, I REALLY WANT TO PLAY THIS GAME. THIS ALONE IS REASON ENOUGH TO BUY AN ENTIRE NINTENDO SWITCH.

Panel 3:
SWITCH

Panel 4:
GOD OF WAR
Only On PlayStation

Panel 5:
HMPH.

Panel 6:
PS4

Panel 7:
Elon Musk @elonmusk
Some of best classic @Atari games coming as Easter eggs in Tesla V9.0 release in about 4 weeks. Thanks @Atari!
SHIT.

Panel 8:
(Stick figure with dollar sign beside a Tesla vehicle)

**Visual note:**
The comic escalates the absurdity of platform exclusives: buying a Switch for one game, frustration over PS4 exclusivity, and finally needing to purchase an entire Tesla car to play classic Atari games.
